LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER JORDAN ST. CLAIRE'S MOTION TO COMPEL UNDERINSURED MOTORIST ARBITRATION. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED, PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH PROCESS OF CCP 1281.6. IF PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO AGREE ON ARBITRATOR, PETITIONER OR RESPONDENT CAN FILE EX PARTE APPLICATION TO HAVE ONE APPOINTED. REQUEST FOR COSTS IS DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; REPORTER: ANN MENDOZA, CSR #6812

calendar.matters:
Motion To Compel Underinsured Motorist Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER JORDAN ST. CLAIRE'S MOTION TO COMPEL UNDERINSURED MOTORIST ARBITRATION. No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted, parties to comply with process of CCP 1281.6. If parties are unable to agree on arbitrator, petitioner or respondent can file ex parte application to have one appointed. Request for costs is denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Reporter: Ann Mendoza, CSR #6812
